sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: In Android development, coroutines must respect the lifecycle of UI components. Instead of using GlobalScope, we use lifecycle-aware scopes provided by Jetpack libraries to automatically manage cancellation.

sections.heading: 1. Lifecycle-Aware Scopes
sections.content: Lifecycle-aware scopes automatically cancel coroutines when their associated lifecycle ends, preventing memory leaks and crashes.

sections.heading: A. lifecycleScope (Activity/Fragment)
sections.content: lifecycleScope is tied to an Activity or Fragment. When the UI component is destroyed, all coroutines inside this scope are cancelled automatically.

sections.heading: B. viewModelScope (Recommended)
sections.content: viewModelScope is tied to a ViewModel. Coroutines survive configuration changes and are cancelled only when the ViewModel is cleared.

sections.heading: 2. Switching Context with withContext
sections.content: Start coroutines on the Main thread and switch to background threads only when needed using withContext.

sections.heading: Switching to IO Thread
sections.content: Use withContext(Dispatchers.IO) to perform heavy work without blocking the UI thread.

sections.heading: 3. Handling Exceptions
sections.content: Always handle exceptions inside coroutines to prevent app crashes.

sections.heading: Using try-catch
sections.content: Wrap risky operations such as network or database calls inside a try-catch block.

sections.heading: 4. repeatOnLifecycle (Modern Collection)
sections.content: repeatOnLifecycle starts collecting data when the lifecycle is at least in a certain state and cancels it when the lifecycle falls below that state.

sections.heading: Collecting Flow Safely
sections.content: Use repeatOnLifecycle when collecting Flow or LiveData to avoid unnecessary background work when the UI is not visible.

sections.heading: 5. Main-Safety
sections.content: A well-written suspend function should be safe to call from the Main thread. It should switch to the appropriate dispatcher internally.

sections.heading: Main-Safe Suspend Function
sections.content: This function safely switches to the IO dispatcher internally, making it safe to call from the Main thread.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Use viewModelScope for business logic and data fetching.
Switch to background threads using withContext(Dispatchers.IO).
Handle exceptions with try-catch inside coroutines.
Use repeatOnLifecycle to safely collect Flow when UI is visible.
Write Main-safe suspend functions to prevent blocking the UI thread.
